meaning: A round fruit that can be red, green, or yellow
example: I eat an apple every day after school.

meaning: A round toy used for playing games
example: Tom kicked the ball into the goal.

meaning: A small furry animal that says meow
example: My cat likes to sleep on the sofa.

meaning: A common pet animal that barks
example: The dog wagged its tail when I came home.

meaning: A round or oval object laid by a hen, used as food
example: Mum fried an egg for my breakfast.

meaning: An animal that lives in water and has fins
example: We saw many colourful fish at the aquarium.

meaning: An animal with wings and feathers that can fly
example: A bird is singing outside my window.

meaning: A very small insect that lives in groups
example: An ant carried a piece of bread back to its home.

meaning: A farm animal with a pink body and curly tail
example: The pig rolled in the mud on the farm.

meaning: A large farm animal that gives us milk
example: The cow ate grass in the field.

meaning: A female chicken that lays eggs
example: The hen laid three eggs this morning.

meaning: A flying insect that makes honey
example: A bee flew from flower to flower in the garden.

meaning: A bird with big eyes that is active at night
example: The owl hooted loudly in the dark.

meaning: A small animal that can jump and lives near water
example: The frog jumped into the pond.

meaning: A bird that can swim and quacks
example: We fed bread to the duck at the park.

meaning: A large wild cat known as the king of the jungle
example: The lion roared loudly at the zoo.

meaning: A large furry wild animal that loves honey
example: The bear found a beehive in the forest.

meaning: A gentle wild animal with long thin legs
example: We saw a deer eating leaves in the forest.

meaning: A sea animal with a hard shell and claws
example: We caught a crab at the beach.

meaning: A small creature with a soft long body that lives in soil
example: A worm crawled out of the wet soil after the rain.

meaning: Small white grains cooked and eaten as food
example: I eat rice with vegetables for lunch every day.

meaning: A white drink that comes from cows and is good for your bones
example: I drink a glass of milk every morning.

meaning: A soft food made from flour and baked in an oven
example: Dad made toast using two slices of bread.

meaning: A sweet baked food often eaten on birthdays
example: Mum baked a chocolate cake for my birthday.

meaning: A hot liquid food made by boiling meat or vegetables
example: Grandma cooked a warm soup for dinner.

meaning: A yellow vegetable with many small seeds on a cob
example: I ate a sweet corn at the school canteen.

meaning: A sweet green or yellow fruit shaped like a teardrop
example: She put a pear in her school bag as a snack.

meaning: A small sour green fruit
example: Mum squeezed a lime into the drink to make it sour.

meaning: A starchy purple or white vegetable
example: I love the yam paste dessert at the hawker centre.

meaning: A small soft round bread
example: I bought a hot cross bun from the bakery.

meaning: A sweet spread made from fruit, put on bread
example: I spread strawberry jam on my toast.

meaning: A hot drink made by pouring water over dried leaves
example: Grandpa drinks a cup of tea every evening.

meaning: Your father
example: Dad took me to the playground after dinner.

meaning: Your mother
example: Mum reads me a bedtime story every night.

meaning: A very young child
example: The baby clapped her hands and smiled at me.

meaning: Your father's or mother's brother
example: Uncle John brought me a toy car as a gift.

meaning: Your father's or mother's sister
example: Aunt Mary made delicious cookies for us.

meaning: A girl who has the same parents as you
example: My sister and I share a room at home.

meaning: A boy who has the same parents as you
example: My brother taught me how to ride a bicycle.

meaning: A person you like and enjoy spending time with
example: My best friend and I sit together in class.

meaning: A male child
example: The boy ran across the field with his kite.

meaning: A female child
example: The girl drew a beautiful picture of her family.

meaning: A grown-up male person
example: The kind man helped me find my lost bag.

meaning: A grown-up female person
example: The woman at the counter gave me a sticker.

meaning: A young person who is not yet grown up
example: Every child in the class got a gold star today.

meaning: A set of pages with words or pictures that you read
example: I borrowed a new book from the school library.

meaning: A tool used for writing with ink
example: Please use a blue pen to write your name.

meaning: A container with handles used to carry things
example: I put my books and water bottle in my school bag.

meaning: A group of pupils who learn together in school
example: Our class is going to the science museum next week.

meaning: A flat stick used to draw straight lines and measure
example: I used my ruler to draw a straight line.

meaning: A thin flat sheet used for writing or drawing
example: The teacher gave each of us a piece of paper.

meaning: A piece of furniture with four legs used for sitting
example: Please sit on your chair and listen to the teacher.

meaning: A flat surface with legs used for eating or working
example: We put our books on the table before class started.

meaning: A table used for studying or writing
example: I keep my pencil case in my desk at school.

meaning: A bright colour like the colour of blood or a fire truck
example: She wore a red dress to the party.

meaning: A colour like the colour of the sky or the sea
example: He coloured the sky blue in his drawing.

meaning: A colour like the colour of grass or leaves
example: The frog sat on a big green leaf.

meaning: A bright colour like the colour of the sun
example: I picked a yellow sunflower from the garden.

meaning: The darkest colour, like the night sky
example: My cat has shiny black fur.

meaning: The lightest colour, like snow or clouds
example: The rabbit had white fluffy fur.

meaning: A light red colour
example: She painted her nails pink for the school concert.

meaning: A colour like the colour of chocolate or wood
example: The dog had soft brown fur.

meaning: A colour between red and yellow, like the fruit
example: He wore an orange shirt on Sports Day.

meaning: A colour made by mixing red and blue
example: She drew purple grapes in her art book.

meaning: The part of your body used for seeing
example: I closed my eye when the bright light shone at me.

meaning: The part of your body used for hearing
example: The music was so loud it hurt my ear.

meaning: The part of your face used for smelling and breathing
example: I could smell the cookies baking through my nose.

meaning: The part of your body at the end of your arm with fingers
example: I raised my hand to answer the teacher's question.

meaning: The long part of your body used for walking
example: My leg was tired after running around the track.

meaning: The part of your body at the end of your leg
example: I hurt my foot when I stepped on a stone.

meaning: The part of your body from your shoulder to your hand
example: She hurt her arm when she fell off the swing.

meaning: The top part of your body where your brain is
example: He nodded his head to say yes.

meaning: The front part of your head with your eyes, nose, and mouth
example: She washed her face before going to bed.

meaning: The soft threads that grow on your head
example: Mum brushed my hair before school.

meaning: One of the hard white parts in your mouth used for biting
example: I lost my first tooth last week.

meaning: The part of your body that connects your head to your body
example: She wore a pretty necklace around her neck.

meaning: The part of your face used for eating and talking
example: Do not talk with food in your mouth.

meaning: To move quickly on your feet
example: The children run to the playground after school.

meaning: To push yourself off the ground into the air
example: She can jump very high on the trampoline.

meaning: To put food in your mouth and swallow it
example: We eat lunch together at the school canteen.

meaning: To swallow a liquid
example: Remember to drink water after exercise.

meaning: To rest with your eyes closed
example: I sleep for nine hours every night.

meaning: To do something fun for enjoyment
example: We play games in the park every evening.

meaning: To move on your feet at a normal pace
example: I walk to school with my mum every morning.

meaning: To say words and speak to someone
example: Please do not talk when the teacher is speaking.

meaning: To make music with your voice
example: We sing the national anthem every Monday at assembly.

meaning: To move your body to music
example: She will dance in the school concert next week.

meaning: To rest with your bottom on a seat
example: Please sit down and take out your books.

meaning: To be on your feet in an upright position
example: Stand up straight when the teacher enters the class.

meaning: To move through water using your arms and legs
example: I learn how to swim at the community pool.

meaning: To travel on something such as a bicycle or bus
example: He loves to ride his bicycle in the park.

meaning: To move through the air
example: We watched the kite fly high in the sky.

meaning: To hit something with your foot
example: He kicked the ball and it went into the goal.

meaning: To press something away from you
example: She had to push the heavy door to open it.

meaning: To move something towards you
example: He had to pull the rope as hard as he could.

meaning: To clean something using water
example: Always wash your hands before you eat.

meaning: To prepare food using heat
example: Dad likes to cook fried rice on weekends.

meaning: To look at words and understand them
example: I read a storybook before bedtime every night.

meaning: To make letters or words on paper
example: We write our names at the top of our worksheets.

meaning: To do something useful for someone
example: I help Mum set the table before dinner.

meaning: To let someone have something
example: She gave her friend half of her orange.

meaning: To pick something up or get something
example: Take out your pencil and start writing.

meaning: To discover something after looking for it
example: I could not find my eraser anywhere in my bag.

meaning: To have something in your hands
example: Hold the handrail when you walk down the stairs.

meaning: To let someone see something
example: She was excited to show her drawing to the class.

meaning: To turn your eyes towards something to see it
example: Look both ways before you cross the road.

meaning: To make a picture using a pencil or crayon
example: He likes to draw animals in his sketchbook.

meaning: Feeling very pleased and joyful
example: She was happy when she got a gold star from her teacher.

meaning: Feeling unhappy or upset
example: He felt sad when he lost his favourite toy.

meaning: Feeling very unhappy and wanting to shout
example: She was angry when her brother broke her crayon.

meaning: Feeling afraid of something
example: The little boy was scared of the loud thunder.

meaning: Being friendly and caring towards others
example: It was kind of her to share her snack with me.

meaning: Feeling pleased about something
example: I am glad that my friend came to visit me.

meaning: Not afraid to do something even when it is hard
example: He was brave enough to try the big waterslide.

meaning: Feeling nervous or uncomfortable around others
example: The new girl was shy on her first day of school.

meaning: The place where you live with your family
example: I go home after school at two o'clock.

meaning: A place where children go to learn
example: I walk to school with my best friend every morning.

meaning: An outdoor area with grass and trees where people relax
example: We played on the swings at the park on Sunday.

meaning: A place where things are sold
example: Mum bought new shoes for me at the shoe shop.

meaning: A place where wild animals are kept for people to see
example: We saw giraffes and elephants at the Singapore Zoo.

meaning: A place where animals are kept and crops are grown
example: We visited a vegetable farm during our school trip.

meaning: A sandy area next to the sea
example: We built sandcastles at the beach during the holidays.

meaning: A long flat surface for vehicles to travel on
example: Look left and right before crossing the road.

meaning: A raised area of land that is not as high as a mountain
example: We climbed up the hill and could see the whole city.

meaning: A small area of water, smaller than a lake
example: There are tortoises living in the pond at the park.

meaning: Water that falls from clouds in the sky
example: We could not play outside because of the heavy rain.

meaning: The bright star in the sky that gives us light and heat
example: The sun was very hot, so I wore my hat.

meaning: Air that moves and blows things around
example: The strong wind blew my kite very high.

meaning: A white or grey mass in the sky made of water droplets
example: Dark clouds appeared before it started to rain.

meaning: Having a high temperature, the opposite of cold
example: Singapore is very hot because it is near the equator.

meaning: Having a low temperature, the opposite of hot
example: The ice cream made my hand feel cold.

meaning: Covered in water or another liquid
example: My umbrella was wet after walking in the rain.

meaning: Not wet or damp
example: I dried my hair with a towel until it was dry.

meaning: Large in size
example: The elephant is a big animal.

meaning: Little in size, not big
example: The ant is a very small insect.

meaning: Moving quickly
example: The cheetah is the fastest animal on land.

meaning: Not moving quickly
example: The tortoise walked in a slow and steady way.

meaning: Greater than normal height
example: The giraffe has a very tall neck.

meaning: Less than normal height or length
example: She is too short to reach the top shelf.

meaning: Great in length from one end to the other
example: The snake was so long it stretched across the path.

meaning: Made or seen for the first time
example: I got a new pencil case for the new school year.

meaning: Having existed for a long time, not new
example: My grandfather has an old photo album at home.

meaning: Free from dirt
example: Make sure your hands are clean before eating.

meaning: Without much light
example: It was dark outside when I woke up early.

meaning: Giving out a lot of light
example: The sun was so bright I had to squint my eyes.

meaning: Making a lot of noise
example: The thunder was so loud that the baby started to cry.

meaning: Not hard, smooth to touch
example: The kitten had very soft fur.

meaning: Not soft, firm and solid
example: The coconut shell is very hard.

meaning: Having a taste like sugar or honey
example: The mango was very sweet and juicy.

meaning: Having a sharp tangy taste like lemon
example: I made a funny face because the lime was too sour.

meaning: Of a high standard, the opposite of bad
example: She got a good grade for her spelling test.

meaning: Not good, causing harm or trouble
example: It is bad manners to talk with your mouth full.

meaning: A clear liquid that we drink and use for washing
example: I drink eight glasses of water every day.

meaning: Things we eat to give our body energy
example: We should eat healthy food to stay strong.

meaning: The energy from the sun or a lamp that lets us see
example: The light in my room helps me read at night.

meaning: The dark part of the day when the sun has gone down
example: I can see many stars in the sky at night.

meaning: The part of the time when there is light from the sun
example: We went to the zoo during the day.

meaning: The passing of moments measured in seconds, minutes, and hours
example: It is time to put away your books and pack your bag.

meaning: The word used to call a person or thing
example: My teacher called my name to answer the question.

meaning: A strong feeling of caring deeply for someone
example: I love my mum and dad very much.

meaning: To do a job or task
example: Dad has to work hard every day at his office.

meaning: A flat surface that opens and closes the entrance to a room
example: Please close the door quietly so you do not wake the baby.

meaning: A tall plant with a trunk, branches, and leaves
example: We rested under the shade of a big tree.

meaning: The colourful part of a plant that blooms
example: She picked a yellow flower from the garden for her mum.

meaning: The short green plants that cover the ground in fields
example: We sat on the grass and had a picnic.

meaning: A large natural stream of water that flows to the sea
example: We walked along the river after dinner.

meaning: The large area of salt water that covers most of the earth
example: I could see the sea from the top of the hill.

meaning: The space above the earth where clouds and stars are
example: The sky turned orange and pink at sunset.

meaning: A tiny bright light seen in the sky at night
example: I counted five stars in the sky before I fell asleep.

meaning: The large round object that shines in the sky at night
example: The full moon was round and bright last night.

meaning: The hot bright flames produced when something burns
example: Do not play with fire because it is very dangerous.

meaning: A small hard piece of rock
example: He threw a stone into the pond and watched the ripples.

meaning: A flat green part of a plant that grows on a branch
example: A yellow leaf fell from the tree into the grass.

meaning: A small object from a plant that grows into a new plant
example: We planted a seed in a pot and watered it every day.

meaning: A vehicle that travels on water
example: We took a boat across the river to reach the island.

meaning: A toy made of a light frame covered with paper, flown in the wind on a string
example: My kite flew so high it looked like a tiny dot in the sky.

meaning: A round musical instrument you hit with sticks
example: He beat the drum loudly during the performance.

meaning: To move towards a place or person
example: Come here and look at this beautiful butterfly.

meaning: To move something so it is not closed
example: Can you help me open this tight bottle cap?

meaning: To not move or continue doing something
example: The bus driver told us to stop running on the bus.

meaning: To move away from a place or towards something
example: It is time to go to bed now.

meaning: To say a question to someone
example: Raise your hand if you want to ask a question.

meaning: To feel that you would like to have something
example: I want to be a doctor when I grow up.

meaning: To have to have something because it is important
example: We need food and water to survive.

meaning: To hold onto something and not give it away
example: You can keep this sticker as a reward.

meaning: In addition to something else, as well
example: I like drawing and I also like reading books.

meaning: Coming right after the present one
example: I will be next in line after her.

meaning: A greater amount or number of something
example: Can I have more rice, please?

meaning: One time only
example: We visit our grandparents once a week on Sundays.

meaning: Used to make the meaning of a word stronger
example: The soup Grandma made was very tasty.

meaning: One more time, happening as before
example: Can you read that story again? I loved it.

meaning: Earlier than the time something happens
example: Brush your teeth before you go to sleep.

meaning: Later than the time something happens
example: We went for ice cream after watching the movie.

meaning: Below or beneath something
example: My cat hid under the bed during the thunderstorm.

meaning: At a higher level than something
example: The bird flew above the trees in the garden.

meaning: Moving to the inside of something
example: The children ran into the classroom when the bell rang.

meaning: A word used to agree or say that something is true
example: Yes, I will help you carry those books.

meaning: A word used to disagree or refuse
example: No, you should not run in the school hallway.

meaning: Only a moment ago, or simply
example: I just finished my homework before dinner.

meaning: Used to say that something is necessary
example: You must wear your seatbelt in the car.

meaning: A large number of things
example: There are many books in the school library.

meaning: Each one of a group without exception
example: Every pupil in my class got a prize today.

meaning: Used to ask about a choice between things
example: Which colour do you want for your new pencil case?

meaning: Belonging to a group of people or things
example: The children put their bags under the desk.

meaning: In or at that place
example: The library is over there, next to the canteen.

meaning: Used to ask about a place
example: Where is my water bottle? I cannot find it.

meaning: Used to ask about a time
example: When will we go to the zoo for our school trip?

meaning: Used to ask for information about something
example: What is your favourite subject in school?

meaning: Used to ask about a person
example: Who is your best friend in class?

meaning: Used to ask for a reason
example: Why are you late to school this morning?

meaning: Used to ask about the way something is done
example: How do you spell your name?

meaning: Belonging to us
example: Our teacher is very patient and kind.

meaning: Used to point to something specific
example: That book on the top shelf is my favourite.

meaning: Used to point to something nearby
example: This is my new eraser that Mum bought for me.

meaning: Used to talk about a group of people or things
example: They played football in the field after school.

meaning: Together or in the company of
example: I go to the library with my classmates every Friday.

meaning: Used to show the starting point of something
example: I got this postcard from my cousin in Japan.

meaning: Belonging to the person being spoken to
example: Is this your pencil on the floor?

meaning: To own or possess something
example: I have a little sister who is two years old.

meaning: Past tense of say, to have spoken words
example: The teacher said we would have a test on Friday.

meaning: Past tense of go, moved from one place to another
example: We went to Sentosa Island during the school holidays.

meaning: Past tense of get, received or obtained something
example: I got full marks for my spelling test.

meaning: Past tense of see, used your eyes to observe something
example: I saw a rainbow after the rain this afternoon.

meaning: Past tense of make, created or produced something
example: She made a pretty card for her teacher's birthday.

meaning: Past tense of come, moved towards a place
example: Dad came home early and took us to the park.
